refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Refining it into sub-FRs focused on monitoring energy use, controlling operations to reduce energy, and optimizing system performance aligns with the energy efficiency KPI and supports verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and comparing to E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy; confirm control actions reduce energy use; demonstrate optimization algorithms improve efficiency.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage.
FR3: Optimize system parameters to improve energy efficiency continuously.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy sensors and data acquisition systems for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive control algorithms for vehicle operation to reduce energy use.
DP3: Use predictive analytics and machine learning for continuous optimization.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring and control modules.
Feedback loops between control and optimization.
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interfaces to minimize complexity.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate sensors and reliable data communication.
Risk of algorithm performance degradation under varying operational conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing optimization separately to allow independent updates and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level integration of energy monitoring, control, and optimization functionalities.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time energy and operational data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands from Teleoperation module to adjust vehicle behavior.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts to Safety Services if energy usage indicates unsafe conditions.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution and external modules for data and control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes energy efficiency functions, minimizing diffusion.
Contains sensors and actuators but limited processing for energy efficiency.
Primarily for remote control, less relevant for energy efficiency logic.
Handles safety alerts but not core energy efficiency functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency logic is best contained within the NGHE Solution module to centralize control and monitoring, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential performance bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes too large.
Dependency on reliable network links for data exchange.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting NGHE Solution into submodules if complexity grows; ensure robust network infrastructure.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched with selected DPs that logically satisfy the energy efficiency requirement with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if optimization algorithms are updated frequently, potentially impacting NGHE Solution module stability.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within NGHE Solution is strong, minimizing cross-module diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and operational testing within the NGHE Solution module boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy efficiency functions to NGHE Solution module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor module size and complexity to avoid performance issues.
Plan for modular updates to optimization algorithms.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment sufficiently addresses the energy efficiency KPI with clear verification and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage.
FR3: Optimize system parameters to improve energy efficiency continuously.

revise.why: No immediate revisions needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

evidence_needed: Performance data of control and optimization algorithms.
